  "description": "\n\t\n\t\t\n\t\tWiFall\n\t\n\nPaper | Code\nThe description is generated by Grok3.\n\n\t\n\t\t\n\t\tSample Usage\n\t\n\nTo run the model, follow these instructions based on the dataset you are using. For the WiGesture Dataset, use the train.py script, and for the WiFall Dataset, use the train_fall.py script. The steps to execute them are the same, and here we provide an example using train.py.\npython train.py --k \u003cshot number> --n \u003cneighbor number for KNN> --p \u003cselect the top p samples from testing set for MK-MMD… See the full description on the dataset page: https:\/\/huggingface.co\/datasets\/RS2002\/WiFall.",

<h2 class="relative group flex items-baseline">
	<a rel="nofollow" href="#sample-usage" class="block pr-1.5 text-lg md:absolute md:p-1.5 md:opacity-0 md:group-hover:opacity-100 md:right-full" id="sample-usage">
		<span class="header-link"><svg viewBox="0 0 256 256" preserveAspectRatio="xMidYMid meet" height="1em" width="1em" role="img" aria-hidden="true" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" class="text-gray-500 hover:text-black dark:hover:text-gray-200 w-4"><path fill="currentColor" d="M167.594 88.393a8.001 8.001 0 0 1 0 11.314l-67.882 67.882a8 8 0 1 1-11.314-11.315l67.882-67.881a8.003 8.003 0 0 1 11.314 0zm-28.287 84.86l-28.284 28.284a40 40 0 0 1-56.567-56.567l28.284-28.284a8 8 0 0 0-11.315-11.315l-28.284 28.284a56 56 0 0 0 79.196 79.197l28.285-28.285a8 8 0 1 0-11.315-11.314zM212.852 43.14a56.002 56.002 0 0 0-79.196 0l-28.284 28.284a8 8 0 1 0 11.314 11.314l28.284-28.284a40 40 0 0 1 56.568 56.567l-28.285 28.285a8 8 0 0 0 11.315 11.314l28.284-28.284a56.065 56.065 0 0 0 0-79.196z"></path></svg></span>
	</a>
	<span>
		Sample Usage
	</span>
</h2>
<p>To run the model, follow these instructions based on the dataset you are using. For the WiGesture Dataset, use the <code>train.py</code> script, and for the WiFall Dataset, use the <code>train_fall.py</code> script. The steps to execute them are the same, and here we provide an example using <code>train.py</code>.</p>
<div class="relative group repo-copy-code"><pre><code class="language-bash">python train.py --k <shot number> --n <neighbor number <span class="hljs-keyword">for</span> KNN> --p <<span class="hljs-keyword">select</span> the top p samples from testing <span class="hljs-built_in">set</span> <span class="hljs-keyword">for</span> MK-MMD (p<1)> --task <action or people> --lr <learning rate>
</code></pre><button class="absolute top-3 right-3 transition opacity-0 group-hover:opacity-80"><svg class="" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" aria-hidden="true" fill="currentColor" focusable="false" role="img" width="0.9em" height="0.9em" preserveAspectRatio="xMidYMid meet" viewBox="0 0 32 32"><path d="M28,10V28H10V10H28m0-2H10a2,2,0,0,0-2,2V28a2,2,0,0,0,2,2H28a2,2,0,0,0,2-2V10a2,2,0,0,0-2-2Z" transform="translate(0)"></path><path d="M4,18H2V4A2,2,0,0,1,4,2H18V4H4Z" transform="translate(0)"></path><rect fill="none" width="32" height="32"></rect></svg></button></div>
<p>Make sure to replace the following placeholders with the appropriate values:</p>
<ul>
<li><code><shot number></code>: Specify the shot number.</li>
<li><code><neighbor number for KNN></code>: Specify the number of neighbors for KNN.</li>
<li><code><select the top p samples from testing set for MK-MMD (p<1)></code>: Specify the value for p (selecting the top p samples from the testing set for MK-MMD). Note that p should be less than 1.</li>
<li><code><action or people></code>: Specify the task name as either "action" or "people".</li>
<li><code><learning rate></code>: Specify the desired learning rate.</li>
</ul>
<p>Once you have set the appropriate values, run the command in your terminal to start the training process.</p>
<h2 class="relative group flex items-baseline">
	<a rel="nofollow" href="#dataset-structure" class="block pr-1.5 text-lg md:absolute md:p-1.5 md:opacity-0 md:group-hover:opacity-100 md:right-full" id="dataset-structure">
		<span class="header-link"><svg viewBox="0 0 256 256" preserveAspectRatio="xMidYMid meet" height="1em" width="1em" role="img" aria-hidden="true" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" class="text-gray-500 hover:text-black dark:hover:text-gray-200 w-4"><path fill="currentColor" d="M167.594 88.393a8.001 8.001 0 0 1 0 11.314l-67.882 67.882a8 8 0 1 1-11.314-11.315l67.882-67.881a8.003 8.003 0 0 1 11.314 0zm-28.287 84.86l-28.284 28.284a40 40 0 0 1-56.567-56.567l28.284-28.284a8 8 0 0 0-11.315-11.315l-28.284 28.284a56 56 0 0 0 79.196 79.197l28.285-28.285a8 8 0 1 0-11.315-11.314zM212.852 43.14a56.002 56.002 0 0 0-79.196 0l-28.284 28.284a8 8 0 1 0 11.314 11.314l28.284-28.284a40 40 0 0 1 56.568 56.567l-28.285 28.285a8 8 0 0 0 11.315 11.314l28.284-28.284a56.065 56.065 0 0 0 0-79.196z"></path></svg></span>
	</a>
	<span>
		Dataset Structure
	</span>
</h2>
<h3 class="relative group flex items-baseline">
	<a rel="nofollow" href="#data-instances" class="block pr-1.5 text-lg md:absolute md:p-1.5 md:opacity-0 md:group-hover:opacity-100 md:right-full" id="data-instances">
		<span class="header-link"><svg viewBox="0 0 256 256" preserveAspectRatio="xMidYMid meet" height="1em" width="1em" role="img" aria-hidden="true" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" class="text-gray-500 hover:text-black dark:hover:text-gray-200 w-4"><path fill="currentColor" d="M167.594 88.393a8.001 8.001 0 0 1 0 11.314l-67.882 67.882a8 8 0 1 1-11.314-11.315l67.882-67.881a8.003 8.003 0 0 1 11.314 0zm-28.287 84.86l-28.284 28.284a40 40 0 0 1-56.567-56.567l28.284-28.284a8 8 0 0 0-11.315-11.315l-28.284 28.284a56 56 0 0 0 79.196 79.197l28.285-28.285a8 8 0 1 0-11.315-11.314zM212.852 43.14a56.002 56.002 0 0 0-79.196 0l-28.284 28.284a8 8 0 1 0 11.314 11.314l28.284-28.284a40 40 0 0 1 56.568 56.567l-28.285 28.285a8 8 0 0 0 11.315 11.314l28.284-28.284a56.065 56.065 0 0 0 0-79.196z"></path></svg></span>
	</a>
	<span>
		Data Instances
	</span>
</h3>
<p>Each instance is a <code>.csv</code> file representing a 60-second sample with the following columns:  </p>
<ul>
<li><strong>seq</strong>: Row number of the entry.  </li>
<li><strong>timestamp</strong>: UTC+8 time of data collection.  </li>
<li><strong>local_timestamp</strong>: ESP32 local time.  </li>
<li><strong>rssi</strong>: Received Signal Strength Indicator.  </li>
<li><strong>data</strong>: CSI data with 104 numbers representing 52 subcarriers, where each subcarrier's complex CSI value is computed as <code>a[2i] + a[2i+1]j</code>.  </li>
<li><strong>Other columns</strong>: Additional ESP32 device information (e.g., MAC, MCS details).</li>
</ul>

	<span>
		Data Splits
	</span>
</h3>
<p>The dataset is organized by person ID (ID0–ID9), with <code>.csv</code> files named after the action performed:  </p>
<ul>
<li><strong>Actions</strong>: fall, jump, sit, stand, walk for 10 individuals (ID0–ID9).</li>
</ul>
<p>Each directory is structured by person ID, with <code>.csv</code> files named after the action performed.</p>

</h2>
<h3 class="relative group flex items-baseline">
	<a rel="nofollow" href="#curation-rationale" class="block pr-1.5 text-lg md:absolute md:p-1.5 md:opacity-0 md:group-hover:opacity-100 md:right-full" id="curation-rationale">
		<span class="header-link"><svg viewBox="0 0 256 256" preserveAspectRatio="xMidYMid meet" height="1em" width="1em" role="img" aria-hidden="true" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" class="text-gray-500 hover:text-black dark:hover:text-gray-200 w-4"><path fill="currentColor" d="M167.594 88.393a8.001 8.001 0 0 1 0 11.314l-67.882 67.882a8 8 0 1 1-11.314-11.315l67.882-67.881a8.003 8.003 0 0 1 11.314 0zm-28.287 84.86l-28.284 28.284a40 40 0 0 1-56.567-56.567l28.284-28.284a8 8 0 0 0-11.315-11.315l-28.284 28.284a56 56 0 0 0 79.196 79.197l28.285-28.285a8 8 0 1 0-11.315-11.314zM212.852 43.14a56.002 56.002 0 0 0-79.196 0l-28.284 28.284a8 8 0 1 0 11.314 11.314l28.284-28.284a40 40 0 0 1 56.568 56.567l-28.285 28.285a8 8 0 0 0 11.315 11.314l28.284-28.284a56.065 56.065 0 0 0 0-79.196z"></path></svg></span>
	</a>
	<span>
		Curation Rationale
	</span>
</h3>
<p>The dataset was created to facilitate research on WiFi-based fall detection, action recognition, and people identification using low-cost ESP32-S3 devices, enabling applications in healthcare, human-computer interaction, and smart environments.</p>
<h3 class="relative group flex items-baseline">
	<a rel="nofollow" href="#source-data" class="block pr-1.5 text-lg md:absolute md:p-1.5 md:opacity-0 md:group-hover:opacity-100 md:right-full" id="source-data">
		<span class="header-link"><svg viewBox="0 0 256 256" preserveAspectRatio="xMidYMid meet" height="1em" width="1em" role="img" aria-hidden="true" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" class="text-gray-500 hover:text-black dark:hover:text-gray-200 w-4"><path fill="currentColor" d="M167.594 88.393a8.001 8.001 0 0 1 0 11.314l-67.882 67.882a8 8 0 1 1-11.314-11.315l67.882-67.881a8.003 8.003 0 0 1 11.314 0zm-28.287 84.86l-28.284 28.284a40 40 0 0 1-56.567-56.567l28.284-28.284a8 8 0 0 0-11.315-11.315l-28.284 28.284a56 56 0 0 0 79.196 79.197l28.285-28.285a8 8 0 1 0-11.315-11.314zM212.852 43.14a56.002 56.002 0 0 0-79.196 0l-28.284 28.284a8 8 0 1 0 11.314 11.314l28.284-28.284a40 40 0 0 1 56.568 56.567l-28.285 28.285a8 8 0 0 0 11.315 11.314l28.284-28.284a56.065 56.065 0 0 0 0-79.196z"></path></svg></span>
	</a>
	<span>
		Source Data
	</span>
</h3>
<ul>
<li><p>Initial Data Collection:</p>
<p>Data was collected in an indoor meeting room with a single transmitter and multiple receivers using ESP32-S3 devices. The setup included:  </p>
<ul>
<li><strong>Frequency Band:</strong> 2.4 GHz  </li>
<li><strong>Bandwidth:</strong> 20 MHz (52 subcarriers)  </li>
<li><strong>Protocol:</strong> 802.11n  </li>
<li><strong>Waveform:</strong> OFDM  </li>
<li><strong>Sampling Rate:</strong> ~100 Hz  </li>
<li><strong>Antenna Configuration:</strong> 1 antenna per device  </li>
<li><strong>Environment:</strong> Indoor with walls and a soft pad to prevent volunteer injuries.</li>
</ul>
</li>
<li><p><strong>Who are the source data producers?</strong>
The data was collected by researchers, with volunteers performing actions in a controlled meeting room environment.</p>
</li>
</ul>
<h3 class="relative group flex items-baseline">
	<a rel="nofollow" href="#annotations" class="block pr-1.5 text-lg md:absolute md:p-1.5 md:opacity-0 md:group-hover:opacity-100 md:right-full" id="annotations">
		<span class="header-link"><svg viewBox="0 0 256 256" preserveAspectRatio="xMidYMid meet" height="1em" width="1em" role="img" aria-hidden="true" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" class="text-gray-500 hover:text-black dark:hover:text-gray-200 w-4"><path fill="currentColor" d="M167.594 88.393a8.001 8.001 0 0 1 0 11.314l-67.882 67.882a8 8 0 1 1-11.314-11.315l67.882-67.881a8.003 8.003 0 0 1 11.314 0zm-28.287 84.86l-28.284 28.284a40 40 0 0 1-56.567-56.567l28.284-28.284a8 8 0 0 0-11.315-11.315l-28.284 28.284a56 56 0 0 0 79.196 79.197l28.285-28.285a8 8 0 1 0-11.315-11.314zM212.852 43.14a56.002 56.002 0 0 0-79.196 0l-28.284 28.284a8 8 0 1 0 11.314 11.314l28.284-28.284a40 40 0 0 1 56.568 56.567l-28.285 28.285a8 8 0 0 0 11.315 11.314l28.284-28.284a56.065 56.065 0 0 0 0-79.196z"></path></svg></span>
	</a>
	<span>
		Annotations
	</span>
</h3>
<ul>
<li><strong>Annotation Process:</strong>
Each <code>.csv</code> file is labeled with the action type (via filename) and person ID (via directory structure). No additional manual annotations were provided.  </li>
<li><strong>Who are the annotators?</strong>
The dataset creators labeled the data based on the experimental setup.</li>
</ul>

	<a rel="nofollow" href="#personal-and-sensitive-information" class="block pr-1.5 text-lg md:absolute md:p-1.5 md:opacity-0 md:group-hover:opacity-100 md:right-full" id="personal-and-sensitive-information">

	</a>
	<span>
		Personal and Sensitive Information
	</span>
</h3>
<p>The dataset includes person IDs (ID0–ID9) but does not contain personally identifiable information such as names or biometric data beyond action and CSI patterns.</p>
